title: Design decision D1
position: Register six built-in contracts in explicit Questions-to-Implementation order, each with a distinct stage-tagged request/result Schema and document or implementation projection
rationale: Preserve stage meaning and deterministic extension without a central stage switch

title: Design decision D2
position: Build one ordered exact-source envelope with Ticket reference first, enabled accepted technical predecessors newest-to-oldest, exact scope/definition/target identities, and contract-local fields
rationale: Bind every request to complete accepted authority without false shared payload semantics

title: Design decision D3
position: Before assembly, compare repository/workflow/Generation/role/revision/final-artifact authority; then read by exact commit/path under a byte cap and verify observed commit, path, blob SHA, and content SHA-256
rationale: Prevent valid Git content from being substituted across repository, workflow, stage, or accepted revision

title: Design decision D4
position: Keep heterogeneous closure storage and type erasure inside `TrustedStageCatalog`; select one trusted registration, decode/bound request and result with its Schemas, invoke only its closures, and decode prepared output
rationale: Restore exact concrete types at one trusted seam and avoid generic-runner stage switches

title: Design decision D5
position: Encode versioned `StageProduceInput` with exact scope, contract ref, complete decoded request, and canonical request hash; load and rehash the exact content-addressed ticket row; replay persisted technical bytes without mutable rediscovery
rationale: Make retry/restart deterministic while keeping every valid stage request within 32 KiB

title: Design decision D6
position: Apply per-read, per-source UTF-8, complete request, configured input, contract-result, prompt/launch, and global result bounds; reject malformed, mistagged, changed, or oversized values before a downstream crossing
rationale: Bound resource use and stop malformed authority or output at the lowest owned boundary

title: Design decision D7
position: CAP-D2 supplies pure currentness expectations, comparison inputs, and typed mismatch diagnostics; CAP-D3/D7 atomically persist/transition only matching work, and CAP-D3/D4/D7 repeat claim/task-exposure fences
rationale: Ensure canonical hashes prove immutability without being mistaken for current authority

title: Design decision D8
position: Prove six-contract order and a seventh registration-only extension; test exact limits, wrong-scope/hash corruption, immutable reads, ticket rows, and outputs locally while leaving transition races and execution/publication/gate/Provenance/capacity lifecycles to their owners
rationale: Supply proportionate evidence without claiming or implementing neighboring lifecycles
